what_it_does: This file is an allocation batch manifest for the interpretive layer: a JSON array that records six design-system substrate files from the claude-ds project, each with project name, relative path, and absolute root directory.
observations: All six entries share project 'claude-ds' and root '/home/tim/company/design/claude-ds'.
The listed paths are preview assets: four HTML overview pages, one JavaScript module, and one markdown prompt file.
Every path sits under the 'preview/' directory.
No code symbols are recorded; symbols[] is empty.
The batch mixes visual documentation, an interactive script, and a prompt artifact without explicit categorization.
standouts: Includes a .prompt.md file as a first-class substrate alongside HTML and JS preview files.
All entries are scoped to a single project root, making this a tightly bounded design-system preview slice.
The JavaScript file is listed but has no extracted symbols.
conventions: Follows a flat JSON-array schema with uniform project/path/root fields per entry.
Uses the file path to encode wave, allocation, and batch identifiers (wave-002/alloc/batch-032).
Treats documentation, script, and prompt files as equivalent ledger substrates.
Breaks the expectation that a JS substrate would have associated code symbols.
concerns: The absolute root path is environment-specific and may not be portable.
No symbols are captured for image-slot.js, leaving its exported API surface undocumented here.
The rationale for grouping these particular six files into batch-032 is not stated.
notes: A small, single-project batch manifest of claude-ds preview artifacts, useful as a scoped input list but light on structural detail.
questions: What criterion determined that these six files belong in batch-032?
Does image-slot.js provide runtime behavior for the listed HTML preview pages?
What does icons_tones.prompt.md generate or document?
Why are no code symbols recorded for the JavaScript entry?
Are the HTML files hand-authored or generated from design tokens?
purpose_vs_actual: The path suggests it should allocate substrates for interpretive processing, and the content does exactly that: it lists claude-ds preview files with provenance metadata.
intention_signals: It appears to capture a curated slice of claude-ds preview and design artifacts so they can be treated as comparable substrates for downstream interpretation or fusion.
novelty: It elevates a .prompt.md file and HTML preview pages to the same ledger status as source code, and records a design-system preview folder rather than component source.
contribution: It preserves a manifest of which preview/design artifacts belong to a given interpretation batch, including project and root provenance for each substrate.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a JSON allocation batch listing six claude-ds design-system preview substrates under /home/tim/company/design/claude-ds, comprising HTML overview pages, a JavaScript image-slot module, and a prompt markdown file, with no recorded code symbols.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to register a scoped set of preview and design artifacts for neutral interpretive ledger processing.
